data.title: REV Group jumps to new all-time high after earnings show off margin improvement
data.description:
REV Group (NYSE:REVG) jumped in Wednesday trading after disclosing that consolidated net sales in the fourth quarter 2024 were $597.9 million, compared to $693.3 million for the three-month period a year ago, which included net sales of $54.2 million attributable to the divested Collins Bus Corporation business. The rest of the revenue decrease for the quarter was primarily due to lower net sales in the Recreational Vehicles segment, partially offset by higher net sales in the Specialty Vehicles segment.

Adjusted EBITDA in the quarter was $49.6 million for the vehicle manufacturer, compared to $54.0 million a year ago. However, the adjusted EBITDA margin rate rose significantly during the quarter.

CEO Mark Skonieczny pointed to significant margin improvement in the Specialty Vehicles segment, as well as "excellent" cost discipline that more than offset the end market demand challenges in the company's cyclical businesses. "In addition, within fiscal 2024, we divested non-core assets, simplified our reporting structure and returned significant cash to shareholders," he added.

REV Group (REVG) said it is entering the new fiscal year with robust earnings momentum, and a commitment to operational excellence and sustainable growth. For the full year, REV Group (REVG) sees net sales in the range of $2.3 billion to $2.4 billion (midpoint $2.35 billion) vs. $2.38 billion consensus, net income in the range of $98.0 to $125.0 million, and adjusted EBITDA in the range of $190.0 million to $220.0 million (midpoint $205 million) vs. $201.2 million consensus.

REV Group (REVG) rallied 16.5% in late morning trading. The stock carved out a new all-time high of $35.96 along the way.

data.title: California Water Service Group rated Buy in new coverage at BofA
data.description:
California Water Service Group (NYSE:CWT) on Wednesday was assigned an investment rating of Buy among analysts at Bank of America. They said the utility is undervalued compared with peer companies.

“California Water (CWT) trades at the lowest multiple of any U.S. pure-play regulated water utility despite improving earnings visibility and meaningfully higher capital investment proposed for 2025-2028,” Ross Fowler, analyst at Bank of America, said in a December 11 report. “The company has excess balance sheet capacity to fund a potential inflection to capital spending.”

BofA set a price target of $57 a share on California Water’s (CWT) stock.

The bank also rated Middlesex Water (MSEX) as Underweight.

data.title: Wells Fargo starts bullishly on Reddit on visible drivers for engagement, monetization
Middlesex Water rated Underperform in new coverage at BofA
data.description:
• Middlesex Water (NASDAQ:MSEX) on Wednesday was rate Underperform in new research coverage by analysts at Bank of America. They said the utility is the most expensive compared with peers, with a price-to-earnings ratio estimated at 23.9 times for 2026, or a 47% premium to the utility group.
  • “General regulatory focus on water customer bill inflation limits further upside, especially as Middlesex (MSEX) becomes reliant on frequent rate cases,” Ross Fowler, analyst at Bank of America, said in a December 11 report.
  • BofA set a price target of $56 a share on Middlesex’s (MSEX) stock.

data.title: Q32 stock craters 68% on mixed Phase 2 data for bempikibart
data.description:
• Shares of Q32 Bio (NASDAQ:QTTB) plunged 68% Wednesday after the company reported mixed Phase 2 results for its drug bempikibart in the treatment of atopic dermatitis, or AD, and alopecia areata, or AA.
  • Q32 Bio (QTTB) said that while a Phase 2a study showed promising clinical activity for the drug in patients AA, the part B portion of a Phase 2a study for AD failed to meet its primary endpoint, according to a statement.
  • Based on the results, the company has decided to proceed with developing the drug for AA but not AD. It plans to enroll around 20 additional patients in a Part B expansion of the Phase 2a study for AA, which will include a loading regimen.
  • The company said it has published an updated investor presentation with details of the drug update on its website.

data.title: H&R REIT stock climbs after RBC upgrades on potential Echo sale
data.description:
H&R Real Estate Investment Trust (HR.UN:CA) stock rose 3.1% in Wednesday morning trading after Royal Bank of Canada upgraded the stock To Outperform from Sector Perform on the potential sale of its stake in Echo Realty.

RBC analyst Jimmy Shan said H&R REIT's 33% stake in Echo is valued at C$1.67 per unit, 18% of current price. "If realized, it would be the largest sale since The Bow and could see the stock respond higher than the +10% move on the sale of Corus Quay in Dec 23, closer to our TP of C$11.50, based on ~30% discount to forward NAV," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

The REIT's stock has "materially disconnected" from its segmented peers, Shan contends, attributing its discount to "scars" from past capital allocation, diversified asset classes, and execution risk in asset sales. "We attribute YTD stock underperformance simply to apathy, in a REIT market characterized by lack of fund flows," the analyst wrote.

data.title: Ripple's stablecoin gets NYDFS approval in 'game-changer' development for XRP
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD), the native cryptocurrency of the XRP Ledger, which was created by Ripple's founders, extended its strong bull run in Wednesday morning trading, surging 20%, after Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse said the private fintech company's much-anticipated stablecoin received final approval from the New York Department of Financial Services.

"This just in…we have final approval from NYDFS for RLUSD! Exchange and partner listings will be live soon," Garlinghouse wrote in a post on X dated Tuesday.

RLUSD marks Ripple's first foray into the $150B space. In April, the company said it's planning to launch its own stablecoin, which will be pegged one-to-one to the U.S. dollar, backed by USD deposits, short-term U.S. Treasuries, and other cash equivalents.

Obtaining final approval from the NYDFS "is a game-changer," Arthur Azizov, CEO of all-in-one crypto ecosystem for business B2BINPAY, said in a statement. "This development is poised to attract even greater investor interest in XRP. Already, RLUSD is being viewed as a potential competitor to USDT and USDC. If, as planned, RLUSD is issued on the XRP Ledger, it could provide an additional boost to XRP's growth."

He sees the price of XRP (XRP-USD) trading in the $5-$7 range during the first half of 2025; at press time, the token changed hands at $2.44, implying a swing of as much as 187%.

data.title: Hershey Trust rejected Mondelez offer as too low - Bloomberg
data.description:
Hershey Co.'s (NYSE:HSY) controlling shareholder, the Hershey Trust, rejected Mondelez's (MDLZ) preliminary offer as too low.

The philanthropic trust, which has ~80% of the voting power at the chocolate maker, rejected the Mondelez (MDLZ) offer, according to a Bloomberg report on Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter. CNBC's David Faber reported the news earlier.

Hershey shares fell 5% on Wednesday after Mondelez (MDLZ) announced a $9 billion share repurchased authorization and said that it's focused on bolt-on acquisitions.

Hershey (HSY) shares jumped 11% on Monday after Bloomberg reported that Mondelez (MDLZ) was evaluating a potential takeover of the chocolate maker. Mondelez made a preliminary approach to Hershey about a possible deal.

"Mondelez always and forever will want to buy Hershey, but the questions is when will the timing be right in terms of the Hershey Trust's willingness to enter sincere negotiations that conceivably would result in a deal," CNBC's David Faber said earlier Wednesday, citing a person involved in the matter.

Hershey rejected a takeover approach from Mondelez (MDLZ) in 2016 as the Hershey Trust was against a transaction.

data.title: S&P, Nasdaq snap two-day decline as latest inflation data cements Fed rate cut bets
data.description:
U.S. stocks were mostly higher on Wednesday, after an in-line consumer inflation report cemented expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) halted a two-day slide each, with the former +0.67% at 6,075.37 points in morning trade and the latter +1.33% at 19,948.86 points. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) wavered, last -0.01% at 44,244.80 points.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was up 2 basis points to 4.24%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was down nearly 1 basis point to 4.14%.

Earlier in the day, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said the consumer price index (CPI) for November rose 0.3% M/M, its highest reading since April and matching the consensus estimate. On a Y/Y basis, headline CPI climbed 2.7%, also matching expectations. Meanwhile, core CPI remained steady for a fourth straight month at +0.3% M/M and came in-line. It increased 3.3% on a Y/Y basis, also in-line.

Ahead of the Fed's final monetary policy committee meeting of the year next week, the CPI data showed that there is still work to do for policymakers in terms of getting inflation back down.

"Today's inflation data probably do not represent a sea change in the outlook for the Federal Reserve. Over the course of 2024, inflation has continued to slow, albeit more gradually than many had hoped it would at the start of the year," Wells Fargo's Sarah House said.

"We believe the FOMC will continue to reduce the federal funds rate next year in an effort to move monetary policy to a less restrictive position. We look for 100 bps of rate cuts from the FOMC over the next 12 months, with 25 bps rate cuts at next week's FOMC meeting and the March, June and September meetings next year," House added.

The U.S. bull run had stalled over the past two days ahead of the November CPI data. Still, Wall Street is on track to end another year with hefty gains.

Wednesday's spotlight was also grabbed by a few key earnings reports. Macy's (M) slid -7.5% after the department store chain delivered a quarterly decline in comparable sales and cut its annual adjusted profit per share guidance.

Heavily shorted GameStop (GME) added +6.3%, after the video game retailer delivered a surprise quarterly profit.

Adobe (ADBE) stock will be in focus ahead of the creative software maker's results after the closing bell.
